commands.review_summary:
The PR changes both `tryMatchModel` candidate sorts to numeric-aware `localeCompare` and adds a `parseModelPattern` regression test for double-digit model versions.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +12. Total +12 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows `parseModelPattern("opus", [claude-opus-4-9, claude-opus-4-10])` reaches the current plain lexicographic comparator, which orders the older `4-9` ahead of `4-10`.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated code repair is needed; the remaining gate is contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance of the compatibility effect.

target.title: fix(model-resolver): use numeric-aware version comparison for model alias resolution
target.body:
Fixes #96588

## What Problem This Solves

`tryMatchModel` sorts candidate model ids with plain lexicographic `localeCompare`, so ids like `claude-opus-4-9` sort above `claude-opus-4-10`. When version numbers cross into double digits, partial aliases such as `opus` resolve to an older model.

## Evidence

### Fix

`src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.ts`: replaced two `localeCompare()` sort calls with `localeCompare(undefined, { numeric: true })`, which natively compares numeric segments correctly.

### Regression test

`src/agents/sessions/model-resolver.test.ts`: added `parseModelPattern("opus", ["claude-opus-4-9", "claude-opus-4-10", "claude-opus-4-11"])` → selects `claude-opus-4-11`.

### Before/after comparison

```
# Old: plain localeCompare
["claude-opus-4-9", "claude-opus-4-10", "claude-opus-4-11"].sort((a,b) => b.localeCompare(a))
→ picks 4-9 ❌

# New: numeric-aware localeCompare
["claude-opus-4-9", "claude-opus-4-10", "claude-opus-4-11"].sort((a,b) => b.localeCompare(a, undefined, {numeric: true}))
→ picks 4-11 ✅
```

### Test verification

```
parseModelPattern("opus", [claude-opus-4-9, claude-opus-4-10, claude-opus-4-11])
→ model.id = "claude-opus-4-11"
```

## Changes

2 files, +15/-23. Lockfile unchanged. Branch based on origin/main.
target.author: zw-xysk
